key_findings: Detector-guided defense preserves 86% of clean accuracy (0.2041/0.2365) vs naive denoising which destroys it (0.1242/0.2365 = 53%)
Neither defense recovers attacked accuracy — simple avg-pool suppression too crude to reverse learned adversarial perturbation
Core value of the detector is in WHEN to suppress (avoiding collateral damage on clean images) not in the suppression technique itself
limitation: Suppression uses simple average-pooling denoising. A learned denoiser or neural purification method would likely recover more attacked accuracy.
future_work: Replace avg-pool suppression with a trained denoising network or adversarial purification module.
